sourceSummary.oneLine: FMEA Report A-TCF-10-105 Rev. 0 for Digital flat panel X-ray detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW, and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management, usability, cybersecurity, IEC 60601-1, EN 62366-1, and RED compliance evidence for the listed digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
keyPoints: The report identifies hazards, hazardous situations, risk controls, verification evidence, and residual risk acceptability.
The document states that residual risks are reduced to the acceptable region after risk control measures are implemented and verified.
Risk controls include labels, user manual information, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, IEC 62304 software process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module.
RED Article 3.1a, Article 3.1b, and Article 3.2 risks are addressed with safety, EMC, and radio spectrum controls.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity access control
concepts.evidence: Restrict random access by setting id / pw when connecting to Wireless network

concepts.name: RED safety requirements
concepts.evidence: Our device must meet safety-related requirements to protect patients and users from potential electrical risks.

concepts.evidence: an adequate level of electromagnetic compatibility as set out in Directive 2014/30/EU.

concepts.name: RED efficient use of radio spectrum
concepts.evidence: Radio equipment shall be so constructed that it both effectively uses and supports the efficient use of radio spectrum in order to avoid harmful interference.

evidence.claim: The report references EN ISO 14971:2019, IEC 60601-1:2006/AMD1:2013, EN 62366-1:2015, and Radio Equipment Directive (RED).

evidence.claim: The detector is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is designed for a service life of approximately 5 years.

evidence.claim: The document states that risk management activities were performed and completed for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector according to the risk management plan, excluding production and post-production activities.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk management activities for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW) according to the risk management plan are performed and completed with appropriately designated personnel exclude the production and post-production related activities.
evidence.pageTarget: Risk Management Process for ME Equipment

evidence.claim: Implemented and verified risk controls include label and user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, cable type differentiation, IEC 62304 software development process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: After implementation and verification of risk controls, residual risks are reduced to the acceptable region and no new hazards requiring additional risk control were identified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the residual risks within the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector are reduced in the acceptable region, and the new hazards needed for additional risk control measures are not identified.

evidence.claim: The detector has a rechargeable battery.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW has the rechargeable battery.

evidence.claim: The detector has Gender cable, AC cord, and LAN cable.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW has Gender cable, AC cord, and Lan cable.

evidence.claim: The detector has a rechargeable battery, AC/DC adapter, and Gender Cable as electrical power sources or related equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW has the rechargeable battery, AC/DC adapter and Gender Cable.

evidence.claim: Software development process was established in compliance with IEC 62304 and software validation was performed.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process was established in compliance with IEC 62304 and software validation was perform.

evidence.claim: Data transmission is made from the device to the PC.
evidence.evidenceText: 14.13,PEMS intended to be incorporated into an IT-Network,A,Data transmission is made from the device to the PC.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity control SRS-C-1 restricts random wireless access by setting ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Restrict random access by setting id / pw when connecting to Wireless network

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity control SRS-C-2 protects transmission data with encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, and WPAPSK+TKIP.
evidence.evidenceText: Transmission data is protected by applying encryption such as WPA, WPAPSK, WPA2, WPA2PSK, WPAPSK+TKIP, etc.

evidence.claim: RED Article 3.1a is addressed as safety-related requirements to protect patients and users from potential electrical risks.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device must meet safety-related requirements to protect patients and users from potential electrical risks.

evidence.claim: RED Article 3.1b is addressed for electromagnetic compatibility.
evidence.evidenceText: Article 3.1b,an adequate level of electromagnetic compatibility as set out in Directive 2014/30/EU.

evidence.claim: RED Article 3.2 is addressed because the device uses radio spectrum to transmit data.
evidence.evidenceText: Article 3.2,Radio equipment shall be so constructed that it both effectively uses and supports the efficient use of radio spectrum in order to avoid harmful interference.,A,Our device uses radio spectrum to transmit data.

evidence.claim: For RED Article 3.2, the risk control is use of an RF module compliant with RED Directive and harmonised standards.
evidence.evidenceText: Using RF Module compliant with RED Directive and harmonised standards.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous because the source appears to be an internal manufacturer FMEA document, while authority/jurisdiction references are standards and EU directives cited within the document.
Preprocessed spreadsheet text contains extraction artifacts and typographical errors such as malformed bullets, repeated comma delimiters, '.A', 'hazzards', 'measeres', and 'indentification'.
Source metadata productSignal is Unclassified even though the source text identifies the product name and model names.
